tools.name: yamllint
tools.run_command: yamllint -d '{extends: default, rules: {line-length: {max: 200}, document-start: disable}}' bundle/ catalog/ config/ .github/ .tekton/ integration-tests/

tools.name: python-yaml-validator
tools.run_command: python3 -c "import yaml, sys, glob; files = glob.glob('**/*.yaml', recursive=True) + glob.glob('**/*.yml', recursive=True); errors = [f for f in files if not any(yaml.safe_load_all(open(f)))]; sys.exit(1 if errors else 0)"

linting.notes: No explicit linter configuration in repository. YAML validation is recommended but not enforced. Some files contain templating syntax ({{productVersion}}, {{scmRevision}}) and will not parse as pure YAML.

testing.notes: This is a configuration repository with no traditional unit or integration tests. Validation occurs through CI pipelines that build container images and run Enterprise Contract checks. Tests are defined in integration-tests/ but execute in external Konflux/Tekton infrastructure.

gating_checks.name: odh-operator-bundle-ci build
gating_checks.command: Tekton PipelineRun triggered via PR label 'build-bundle' or 'build-all' or comment '/build-konflux bundle'. References pipeline from https://github.com/opendatahub-io/odh-konflux-central.git (pipeline/bundle-build.yaml)

gating_checks.name: odh-fbc-fragment-ci build
gating_checks.command: Tekton PipelineRun triggered via PR label 'build-catalog' or 'build-all' or comment '/build-konflux catalog'. References pipeline from https://github.com/opendatahub-io/odh-konflux-central.git (pipeline/multi-arch-catalog-build.yaml)

gating_checks.name: enterprise-contract
gating_checks.command: Tekton task verify-enterprise-contract validates built container images against policy

ci.notes: CI is Tekton-based and requires Konflux infrastructure (OpenShift cluster with Tekton Pipelines). PR checks are triggered by labels or slash commands. GitHub Actions workflows process bundle/catalog changes on main branch and sync from upstream operator repo. Build pipelines reference external configs from opendatahub-io/odh-konflux-central and red-hat-data-services/RHOAI-Konflux-Automation.

build.build_command: podman build -f bundle/Dockerfile -t bundle:latest . && podman build -f catalog/v4.20/Dockerfile -t catalog:latest catalog/v4.20/
build.notes: Bundle Dockerfile is FROM scratch and packages manifests/metadata/tests. Catalog Dockerfile is FROM registry.redhat.io/openshift4/ose-operator-registry-rhel9:v4.20 and runs opm serve. No traditional build/install steps required for local validation - this is a config-only repository.

conventions.notes: YAML configuration files follow Kubernetes/OpenShift operator conventions. Bundle manifests in bundle/manifests/, catalog in catalog/v4.20/, Tekton configs in .tekton/. Files use multi-document YAML (--- separators) in some cases (catalog.yaml). Template files contain {{variable}} placeholders processed by external Python scripts.

lint_commands.command: yamllint -d '{extends: default, rules: {line-length: {max: 200}, document-start: disable}}' bundle/manifests/*.yaml
lint_commands.notes: Produces indentation errors in CRD files - these are auto-generated and not hand-edited. Linter finds issues but files are syntactically valid YAML.

lint_commands.command: python3 -c "import yaml, glob; files = glob.glob('bundle/manifests/*.yaml'); [yaml.safe_load(open(f)) for f in files]; print(f'Parsed {len(files)} files')"
lint_commands.notes: Successfully parses all bundle manifest YAML files (32 files).
Successfully parses multi-document catalog YAML (3 documents).

container_recipe.notes: This is a config-only repository. Validation is limited to YAML syntax checking. No traditional tests exist. The container recipe validates YAML parsing only.

results.command: apt-get update -qq && apt-get install -y -qq yamllint && pip install PyYAML
results.notes: Dependencies installed cleanly in python:3.11-slim container

results.command: yamllint -d '{extends: default, rules: {line-length: {max: 200}, document-start: disable}}' bundle/manifests/*.yaml
results.output_snippet:
bundle/manifests/components.platform.opendatahub.io_codeflares.yaml
  17:3      error    wrong indentation: expected 4 but found 2  (indentation)
  18:5      error    wrong indentation: expected 6 but found 4  (indentation)
results.notes: yamllint finds indentation issues in CRD files. These are auto-generated and syntactically valid - yamllint is too strict for auto-generated CRDs.
All bundle manifests parse successfully as valid YAML despite yamllint warnings.

validation.summary: YAML syntax validation passes. yamllint produces warnings on auto-generated CRDs but files are valid. No traditional tests to run.

gaps: No traditional unit or integration tests - validation is CI-only
CI pipelines require Konflux/Tekton infrastructure not accessible locally
Python processing scripts (bundle-processor.py, fbc-processor.py) are in external repository (red-hat-data-services/RHOAI-Konflux-Automation)
Enterprise Contract checks require container images to be built first
Some configuration files are templates with {{variable}} syntax and cannot be parsed as pure YAML
No local validation beyond YAML syntax checking
Bundle sync workflow depends on external opendatahub-operator repository
